dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: Right hand. I need it to heal faster. I use my right hand for everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: The patch might fall off from constant movement, but at least this way, the hand I need the most gets what it needs.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She carefully applies the patch to her right hand, securing it as best she can. It feels like armor—small, fragile armor.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: And my left hand gets the strong medicine.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She dips her left hand into the honey and garlic mixture. The sting is immediate and sharp—a gasp escapes her.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: Ah—! Ngh, that's…

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: Hot. Sharp. But… it means it's working, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She sits back, breathing through the sting. Both hands are treated now—one gently, one harshly. The duality of her survival.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I need to sleep. The day starts so early…

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She lies down on her thin bed. The pillow is small. The blanket worn. She closes her eyes and tries to find rest.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: But sleep is a luxury. Minutes turn to an hour. The darkness pressing down feels suffocating. Every small sound outside makes her tense.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I can't stay here. I can't breathe in this room anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: If I don't leave, I think I'll just… disappear.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She's on her feet before logic catches up. Her hands throb—one patched, one burning with the medicine. Neither is truly comfortable. She moves anyway.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: Outside. I need to go outside.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: The early morning streets are quiet. A few puddles reflect the streetlamps. Claire walks with no destination, just a need to escape the four walls that have held her captive.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: At 4:15 AM, she stops. A shophouse glows softly ahead. Through the window, a man works—sleeves rolled, methodical, gentle—forming tarts with skilled hands. Above: 'SUPPER WEEKEND til 5:00 AM'.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: Still open… and it looks…

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: The café looks warm. Welcoming. Like a place where people take care of small things—and by extension, each other.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I need this. Whatever this is.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [35 minutes before closing, she pushes open the door. The chime is soft. The man inside looks up—and his gaze is kind.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: Welcome. Come in, come in.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: Kit sees her immediately—damp, trembling, hurting. His eyes find her hands: the patch on one, the stains of garlic on the other. He recognizes the signs of someone who knows how to tend their own wounds.

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: What brings you here at this hour? You look like you've been through something.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I saw the light. I couldn't stay where I was.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [Kit nods as if this makes perfect sense. He sets down a warm drink—something deeper than coffee, richer, more restorative. Then he steps back to the kitchen.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: When he returns, he brings ten small tarts—golden, perfect, still warm. And a bottle: iodine solution, the kind that truly heals deep wounds.

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: This place is the Wish House. You're welcome here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: And these are for someone who's learned to care for themselves the hard way. Garlic is strong, but you deserve stronger kindness now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: How did you…?

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: You don't need to understand. Just rest. Let the world wait for once.

cafeOrders.comments: Kit provides both physical healing and emotional sanctuary.

items.description: Iodine solution for proper wound healing
